titleStmt.title: Mr. Dayton to to Mr. Seward

div.head: Mr. Dayton to Mr. Seward
div.p: Sir: I send you the enclosed slips, (one cut from a French paper of yesterday, and the other cut from Galignani of this morning,) the first of which announces the sailing of a first detachment of six hundred men of the Belgian legion destined to Mexico, and the other the contemplated conveyance to Yera Cruz of four thousand two hundred volunteers enlisted in Austria. The enlistments in Europe for service in Mexico, it would seem from this and other notices I have observed, are quite prompt.
I am, sir, your obedient servant,

frus:attachment.head: [Extract. —Translation]
frus:attachment.p: In a despatch from St. Nazaire we noticed that the Louisiana, of the General Transatlantic Company, went to sea yesterday at three o’clock. She took the first detachment of six hundred men of the Belgian legion enrolled for Mexico, one hundred and seventy-eight civilian passengers, and a full cargo of merchandise and material exported for carrying on of the war.

General Count de Thun has arrived in Paris, charged by the Mexican government to come to an arrangement with the Transatlantic Steam Navigation Company for the conveyance to Yera Cruz of four thousand two hundred volunteers, enlisted in Austria.
